summary: CLI flags parser rejects negative prefetch depth without clear message
description: Running the CLI with --prefetch-depth -1 crashes with a raw stack trace instead of a helpful error. Expected the same clean validation message we get from other bad flag values.

comments.author: Jonah Kim
comments.body: Confirmed, parseFlags in src/cli/flags.ts checks Number.isFinite and value < 1 but Number.parseInt("-1") passes both, so the malformed check never trips for negative numbers specifically. Wait, actually the bug is the finite check runs before we realize -1 also fails value < 1, but the error message path wasn't hit because of an operator precedence slip.

comments.author: Jonah Kim
comments.body: Root cause was simpler: the check was written correctly but a test stub earlier in the call chain swallowed the thrown error and re-threw a generic one. Fixed the swallow, negative values now surface the intended message.
